name: APT41 Intrusion — CrowdStrike Investigation
description: MITRE ATT&CK layer mapping observed APT41 (Wicked Panda) TTPs detected during a CrowdStrike Falcon endpoint triage and Next-Gen SIEM investigation on host 5236A4. Each technique reflects a confirmed detection or log finding. Techniques are color-coded by tactic phase.

techniques.comment: CONFIRMED — Multiple UserLogonFailed events detected on host 5236A4 via CrowdStrike Next-Gen SIEM. Query: #event_simpleName contains UserLogonFailed AND ComputerName = 5236A4. Consistent with APT41's documented password spraying against RDP and local admin accounts.

CONFIRMED (sub-technique) — Password guessing/spraying inferred from high-volume UserLogonFailed events. Likely targeting built-in Administrator account. APT41 documented use of brute force for initial RDP access.
CONFIRMED (parent) — Event Triggered Execution via Accessibility Features hijack. CrowdStrike flagged IFEO registry modification. See sub-technique T1546.008.

CONFIRMED — CrowdStrike Falcon Detection: Registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\sethc.exe modified. Debugger value set to C:\windows\system32\cmd.exe. Enables pre-authentication SYSTEM shell via RDP Sticky Keys (5x Shift). Tactic: Persistence.

CONFIRMED (x2 instances) — certutil.exe abused as LOLBin to download remote files on host 5236A4. First instance: downloaded script.ps1. Second instance: dropped Isass.exe (malicious masqueraded executable). Classic APT41 ingress tool transfer technique.
CONFIRMED (parent) — Scripting interpreter used for payload execution. See sub-technique T1059.001 (PowerShell).

CONFIRMED — script.ps1 executed with -DumpCreds parameter. Identified via Next-Gen SIEM event search: Any field contains script.ps1 AND ComputerName = 5236A4. PowerShell used as execution wrapper for Mimikatz credential dumping. Tactic: Credential Access.
CONFIRMED (parent) — Credential dumping activity observed. See sub-technique T1003.001 (LSASS Memory).

CONFIRMED — script.ps1 executed with -DumpCreds parameter, consistent with Mimikatz LSASS memory dumping. Detected via CrowdStrike Next-Gen SIEM. Tactic: Credential Access. All credentials on host 5236A4 should be treated as compromised.
CONFIRMED (parent) — Masquerading technique used. See sub-technique T1036.005.

CONFIRMED — File Isass.exe (capital I substituting lowercase L) dropped to C:\Windows\System32\ to impersonate legitimate lsass.exe Windows process. Detected as new file on disk via second certutil.exe Falcon detection. SIEM query: CommandLine contains Isass.exe AND ComputerName = 5236A4.
CONFIRMED (parent) — Malicious Windows service created. See sub-technique T1543.003.

CONFIRMED — Isass.exe (masqueraded executable) registered as a Windows service with path C:\\Windows\\System32\\Isass.exe. Provides persistent execution on every boot. Detected via CrowdStrike Next-Gen SIEM event search on CommandLine field for host 5236A4.
